meaning: adv.不能取消地,不能撤回地
examples: My relationships with friends have been irrevocably altered by their reactions to my illness.
The events with Treya changed me deeply, profoundly, irrevocably.
Watching Late Night Gimp Fight made me think I had irrevocably crossed a line.
When the perpetrator of abuse is a priest a sacred trust has been irrevocably betrayed.
What's certain, however, is that the contest over reform in Indonesia has been irrevocably radicalized.
